    The text does not explicitly include any equations. However, the text discusses using Chemkin to model combustion and predict emissions levels. It mentions that by changing variables such as catalysts, temperature, and pressure, the model can predict the amount and timing of emissions formation, implying the underlying presence of complex chemical kinetics equations. A common equation used in combustion simulations (though not found on the page) relates to NOx formation; for example, Zeldovich Mechanism NOx = k * T^n * exp(-E/RT)
